_comment: Single source of truth for the relay rules, shared by relay.mjs and every agent session. 'allowed' is the AUTHORITATIVE permission list: an edge is permitted iff it is listed here. 'chain' documents the linear pipeline order (the chain agents have only neighbour edges in 'allowed', so their adjacency is enforced implicitly). The owner<->interpreter edge is exercised in the Interpreter's live session with the human, not via mailbox, but is recorded here so its messages still validate when written to the ledger. The SENTINEL stands outside the chain and, via the sentinel>* edges below, may speak one-way to ANY agent (advisory/warning/directive) to keep communication on-contract; no agent replies to it. The QA reviewer also stands outside the chain: via the qa>builder edge it sends one-way test-design reviews (Farley Index quality of the Builder's tests) to the Builder, who does not reply. A 'broadcast' is an EXTRAORDINARY message the Owner can send to pass an instruction down the WHOLE line: it is allowed on every downward edge (owner>interpreter>analyst>examiner>builder) and each agent applies it then forwards it on, unchanged in intent, to its downstream neighbour.
